page: Amazon Web Services vt1.6xlarge is a video transcoding-focused server instance powered by the Nitro hypervisor. It features an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 24 vCPUs and 12 physical cores operating at a clock speed of 2.5 GHz. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including AVX, AVX-512 Foundation, and AVX-512 Vector Neural Network Instructions (VNNI). The system is equipped with 48.0 GB of DDR4 memory running at 3 GHz, providing 2.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware, and it offers a baseline network bandwidth of 6 Gbps.

Benchmark data indicates that the vt1.6xlarge delivers average multi-core performance but exhibits weak single-core capabilities. In stress-ng testing, single-core CPU performance falls into the bottom 10%, while multi-core performance sits in the middle 50%. Similarly, Geekbench single-core and text processing scores are relatively weak, whereas multi-core, ray tracing, and software compilation benchmarks achieve average ratings. Memory bandwidth tests for both cached and uncached reads and writes consistently rank in the middle 50%.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the vt1.6xlarge offers a specialized compute-to-memory ratio of 1:2, making it a resource-dense option for multi-threaded workloads that do not require massive memory footprints or local storage. Given its hardware profile and average multi-core performance, this instance is designed for video transcoding, multi-threaded decompression, and moderate web serving or database operations. It is less suited for single-threaded applications or memory-intensive database tasks.
description: Amazon Web Services vt1.6xlarge is a Nitro-based server instance designed for video transcoding workloads. It is equipped with an Intel Xeon 8259CL processor featuring 24 vCPUs, 12 physical cores, and a 2.5 GHz clock speed on an x86_64 architecture. The instance includes 48.0 GB of DDR4 memory, providing a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. Network performance is anchored by a 6 Gbps baseline bandwidth. Benchmarks show average multi-core CPU and memory bandwidth performance, contrasted by weak single-core execution. This hardware profile makes the vt1.6xlarge qualitatively cost-efficient for multi-threaded tasks such as video transcoding, multi-core software compilation, and moderate database operations, while remaining less optimal for single-threaded or memory-heavy applications.
og_description: Amazon Web Services vt1.6xlarge is a Nitro-based server featuring 24 vCPUs, 48 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU. It is designed for video transcoding, parallel processing, and multi-threaded workloads.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services vt1.6xlarge features 24 vCPUs, 48 GB of DDR4 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, optimized for video transcoding and multi-threaded workloads.
tagline: A multi-core compute instance featuring a 1:2 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, optimized for video transcoding and parallel processing workloads.
bullet_points: Intel Xeon 8259CL processor with 24 vCPUs and 12 physical cores
48.0 GB of DDR4 memory with a compute-focused 1:2 resource ratio
6 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the AWS Nitro hypervisor
Supports AVX-512 and VNNI instruction sets for vector operations
Optimized for video transcoding and multi-threaded processing tasks
No local storage or dedicated GPU hardware included
